BreakEvenAnalysis.Context: Pre-launch model for the Off-Mic podcast supporter programme and merchandise shop. Built ahead of the Pernod Ricard launch sponsorship to confirm the Q3 spend plan is recoverable. Numbers in ZAR.

CurrentState.Note: Conservative pre-launch model. SARS provisional tax of about R 7,200/mo is tracked separately by Thandi. Rate column holds the Patreon supporter tier price (R 95/mo). InstructorPay_Raj line covers the freelance Off-Mic producer retainer (Raj Pillay, Bryanston). Revisit after the Pernod Ricard launch month closes.

Scenario_A_RentTo750.Label: Bryanston Sound studio hire bumps the monthly studio bill from R 9,000 to R 10,500
Scenario_A_RentTo750.BreakEvenCalculation: Fixed costs R 40,155 / Patreon tier R 95 = 422.7 -> 423 supporters minimum to cover all fixed costs without other revenue.
Scenario_A_RentTo750.Impact: Negligible. Sponsorship and merch revenue keep the business comfortably profitable; supporter count stays well above the threshold.

Scenario_B_RentTo850.Label: Stress test - studio hire jumps to R 12,500/mo (Bryanston peak booking pressure)
Scenario_B_RentTo850.Impact: Still healthy. If sustained for two quarters Asha and Thandi will renegotiate with Bryanston or shift one weekly session to the spare home setup in Melville.

BreakEvenAnalysis.KeyInsight: Break-even is around 423-444 Patreon supporters at the current cost base, but live revenue runs at 248 supporters plus 12 active brand sponsorships, so the business is sitting on R 12,055/mo of retained reserves earmarked for the Off-Mic launch. Main exposure is concentration risk if Pernod Ricard or Nedbank churn together.
ActionItems: Confirm Pernod Ricard launch sponsorship paper-trail with Palesa before 2026-06-15
Stress-test Scenario B against actuals one month after the Off-Mic launch (target review 2026-08-25)
Lock the Bryanston Sound session rate in writing through 2027-03-31 ahead of the budget cycle
